REPLY TO DEFT'S OPPOSITION TO PLNTF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FILED BY PLAINTIFF HIDAS, IBAY

DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: REUBEN J. DONIG FOR PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY: IBAY HIDAS ANNMARIE M. LIERMANN FOR DEFENDANT AND OPPOSING PARTY: SAFEWAY STORES INC. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE STATEMENTS MADE BY THE PLAINTIFF TO JOSEPH MASCITTO AS SET FORTH IN THE ACCIDENT AND INCIDENT REPORT ARE NOT SUBJECT TO THE ATTORNEY - CLIENT PRIVILEGE PURSUANT TO HOLM V. SUPERIOR COURT (1954) 42 CAL. 2D 500. ANY OBSERVATIONS, IMPRESSIONS, OR CONCLUSIONS OF MR. MASCITTO CONTAINED IN THE REPORTS ARE PRIVILEGED AND NEED NOT BE DISCLOSED. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO SUBMIT THE REPORTS OR PORTIONS OF THE REPORTS THAT CONTAIN THE STATEMENTS OF PLAINTIFF TO THE COURT FOR AN IN CAMERA REVIEW AS TO THE APPLICABILITY OF THE ATTORNEY WORK - PRODUCT PRIVILEGE. ALL REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. THIS MATTER IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 18, 2005 AT 9:00AM. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
